item.title: 如何在 Ubuntu 20.04 上安装 LAMP（Linux Apache Mysql PHP） | How to Install LAMP(Linux Apache Mysql PHP) on Ubuntu 20.04
item.description:
坦梅舒克拉

发表于组件名称的第一个字母组成了 LAMP 的首字母缩写词：

这个术语实际上是一个首字母缩写词，代表Linux操作系统和Apache Web 服务器。 站点数据存储在MySQL数据库中，动态内容由PHP处理。

下图显示了图层如何堆叠在一起：

步骤 1 安装 Apache 并更新防火墙。

首先，我们需要在我们的虚拟机上安装 apache，但在此之前让我们通过以下命令更新必要的软件。 提醒一下，ubuntu 使用 apt 包管理器，而基于 Red hat 的发行版（如 centos）使用 yum 包管理器。

 $sudo apt install apache2

现在您需要更新防火墙，以便您的防火墙允许 HTTP 和 HTTPS 流量。 检查 UFW（简单防火墙），这是一个运行在 iptables 之上的防火墙配置工具，默认包含在 Ubuntu 发行版中。

 Output Available applications: Apache Apache Full Apache Secure OpenSSH

如果您查看 Apache Full 配置文件详细信息，您会看到它启用了到端口 80 和 443 的流量：

 sudo ufw app info "Apache Full"
 Output Profile: Apache Full Title: Web Server (HTTP,HTTPS) Description: Apache v2 is the next generation of the omnipresent Apache web server. Ports: 80,443/tcp

现在要允许此服务器的传入 HTTP 和 HTTPS 流量，请运行：

 sudo ufw allow "Apache Full"
 OUTPUT Rules updated Rules updated (v6)

然后检查，转到您的实例公共 IP 地址

注意：要知道您的 IP 地址，我们可以使用以下命令：

 $ sudo apt install curl $ curl http://icanhazip.com

它将显示带有 apache 信息的类似内容：

步骤 2 安装 MYSQL。

现在我们的网络服务器已经启动并运行，是时候安装 Mysql 了。

 $ sudo apt install mysql-server

此命令将向您显示将要安装的软件包的列表，以及它们将占用的磁盘空间量。 输入 Y 继续。 安装完成后，运行一个简单的安全脚本来删除一些危险的默认设置并锁定对数据库系统的访问。

 $ sudo mysql_secure_installation

现在阅读并完成密码设置等安装步骤。
完成后测试您是否能够登录到 mysql 控制台

回答 Y 表示是，或者任何其他内容在不启用的情况下继续。

 VALIDATE PASSWORD PLUGIN can be used to test passwords and improve security. It checks the strength of password and allows the users to set only those passwords which are secure enough. Would you like to setup VALIDATE PASSWORD plugin? Press y|Y for Yes, any other key for No:

如果您启用了密码验证，您将看到您刚刚输入的根密码的密码强度，并且您的服务器会询问您是否要更改该密码。 如果您对当前密码感到满意，请在提示符处输入 N 表示“否”：

 Using existing password for root. Estimated strength of the password: 100 Change the password for root ? ((Press y|Y for Yes, any other key for No) : n

最后输入以下内容进入mysql控制台

Output Welcome to the MySQL monitor. Commands end with ; or \g. Your MySQL connection id is 5 Server version: 5.7.34-0ubuntu0.18.04.1 (Ubuntu) Copyright (c) 2000, 2021, Oracle and/or its affiliates. Oracle is a registered trademark of Oracle Corporation and/or its affiliates. Other names may be trademarks of their respective owners. Type 'help;' or '\h' for help. Type '\c' to clear the current input statement. mysql>

并输入


离开控制台。

步骤 3 安装 PHP

为什么？ 安装它。 因为 PHP 可以运行脚本，连接到您的 MySQL 数据库以获取信息，并将处理后的内容交给您的 Web 服务器，以便它可以将结果显示给您的访问者。 键入以下命令：

 $ sudo apt install php libapache2-mod-php php-mysql

我们在上面的命令中安装了三个包：

  • 要扩展 PHP 的功能，您可以选择安装一些附加模块。

    使用箭头键上下滚动，然后按 Q 退出。

    结果是您可以安装的所有可选组件。 它会给你一个简短的描述：

    例如，要找出 php-cli 模块的作用，您可以输入：

    如果你认为 php-cli 是你需要的东西，你可以输入：

    第 4 步在您的 Web 服务器上测试 PHP 处理

    要测试您的系统是否为 PHP 正确配置，请创建一个名为info.php的 PHP 脚本。 为了让 Apache 找到这个文件并正确地提供它，它必须被保存到你的 web根目录。

     $ sudo nano /var/www/**your_domain**/info.php

    插入以下命令以显示 php 信息。

    保存并关闭它您要访问的地址是：

     http://your_domain/info.php

    你来到的页面应该是这样的：

    现在您已经安装了 LAMP 堆栈，接下来您有很多选择。 您已经安装了一个平台，该平台允许您在服务器上安装大多数类型的网站和 Web 软件。

    阿布舍克·施利瓦斯塔瓦—— 一旦暂停，tanmaygi 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，tanmaygi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，tanmaygi 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 tanmaygi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    Tanmay Shukla

    Posted on Jul 14

    ## What is LAMP Stack? The LAMP stack is a popular open-source solution stack used primarily in web development. LAMP consists of four components necessary to establish a fully functional web development environment. The first letters of the components' names make up the LAMP acronym:

    This term is actually an acronym which represents the Linux operating system, with the Apache web server. The site data is stored in a MySQL database, and dynamic content is processed by PHP.

    The illustration below shows how the layers stack together:

    Step-1 Install Apache and Update the Firewall.

    First we need to install apache on our Virtual machine but before that lets update the necessary softwares by following command. Just to remind, ubuntu used apt package manager and Red hat based distros like centos uses yum package manager.

    $sudo apt install apache2

    Now you need to update the firewall so that your firewall allow HTTP and HTTPS traffic. To check the UFW (uncomplicated firewall) which is a firewall configuration tool that runs on top of iptables, included by default within Ubuntu distributions.

    Output
Available applications:
  Apache
  Apache Full
  Apache Secure
  OpenSSH

    If you look at the Apache Full profile details, you’ll see that it enables traffic to ports 80 and 443:

    sudo ufw app info "Apache Full"

    Output
Profile: Apache Full
Title: Web Server (HTTP,HTTPS)
Description: Apache v2 is the next generation of the omnipresent Apache web
server.

Ports:
  80,443/tcp

    Now to allow incoming HTTP and HTTPS traffic for this server, run:

    sudo ufw allow "Apache Full"

    OUTPUT

Rules updated
Rules updated (v6)

    Then to check , go to your instance public IP address

    Note: To know your IP address,we can use following commands:

    $ sudo apt install curl
$ curl http://icanhazip.com

    It will show something like this with apache information:

    Step-2 Installing MYSQL.

    As we have now our webserver up and running, its time to install Mysql.

    $ sudo apt install mysql-server

    This command will show you a list of the packages that will be installed, along with the amount of disk space they’ll take up. Enter Y to continue. When installation is done, run a simple security script to remove some dangerous defaults and lock down access to your database system.

    $ sudo mysql_secure_installation

    Now read and complete the installtion steps like password set etc.
    When it's done test if you are able to logged in to the mysql console

    Answer Y for yes, or anything else to continue without enabling.

    VALIDATE PASSWORD PLUGIN can be used to test passwords
and improve security. It checks the strength of password
and allows the users to set only those passwords which are
secure enough. Would you like to setup VALIDATE PASSWORD plugin?

Press y|Y for Yes, any other key for No:

    If you’ve enabled password validation, you’ll be shown the password strength for the root password you just entered and your server will ask if you want to change that password. If you are happy with your current password, enter N for “no” at the prompt:

    Using existing password for root.

Estimated strength of the password: 100
Change the password for root ? ((Press y|Y for Yes, any other key for No) : n

    Finally type the following to enter into mysql console

    Output
Welcome to the MySQL monitor.  Commands end with ; or \g.
Your MySQL connection id is 5
Server version: 5.7.34-0ubuntu0.18.04.1 (Ubuntu)

Copyright (c) 2000, 2021, Oracle and/or its affiliates.

Oracle is a registered trademark of Oracle Corporation and/or its
affiliates. Other names may be trademarks of their respective
owners.

Type 'help;' or '\h' for help. Type '\c' to clear the current input statement.

mysql>

    and type


    to get out of the console.

    Step-3 Installing PHP

    Why? to install it. Because PHP can run scripts, connect to your MySQL databases to get information, and hand the processed content over to your web server so that it can display the results to your visitors. Type the below command:

    $ sudo apt install php libapache2-mod-php php-mysql

    We've installed three packages in the above command:

    • php package
    • libapache2-mod-php to integrate PHP into Apache webserver.
    • php-mysql package to allow PHP to connect to MySQL databases.

    To extend the functionality of PHP, you have the option to install some additional modules.

    Use the arrow keys to scroll up and down, and press Q to quit.

    The results are all optional components that you can install. It will give you a short description for each:

    For example, to find out what the php-cli module does, you could type this:

    If you decided that php-cli is something that you need, you could type:

    Step-4 Test the PHP Processing on your Web Server

    To test that your system is properly configured for PHP, create a PHP script called info.php. In order for Apache to find this file and serve it correctly, it must be saved to your web root directory.

    $ sudo nano /var/www/**your_domain**/info.php

    Insert the following command to show the php information.

    Save and close it The address you will want to visit is:

    http://your_domain/info.php

    The page that you come to should look something like this:

    Now that you have a LAMP stack installed, you have many choices for what to do next. You’ve installed a platform that will allow you to install most kinds of websites and web software on your server.

    Abhishek Shrivastava - Jun 10

    Once suspended, tanmaygi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, tanmaygi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by tanmaygi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If tanmaygi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: tanmaygi

item.title: 你应该知道的 PhpStorm 插件 | PhpStorm Plugins You Should Know
item.description:
如果您是 PhpStorm 用户，您就会知道它具有许多使 PHP 编程更快、更容易的强大功能。 除此之外，JetBrains 市场提供了许多有用的插件，旨在提高我们的生产力和工作舒适度。 我决定测试其中一些，这里是我选择的真正有影响的工具！

这个插件是一个开源的 PHP 代码审查和静态代码分析工具（也就是 JetBrains IDE 中的检查），它似乎比原生检查更有效。 它是 PhpStorm 的必备插件，以确保高质量的编码。 默认配置非常严格，但由于这一点，您可以避免很多代码异味（或禁用一些您不喜欢的规则）。

其中包括：与架构相关的问题、弱类型控制和可能的代码构造简化、性能问题、非最优、重复和可疑的“如果”条件、魔术方法使用的验证等等！

这个插件绝对是增强 PhpStorms 的内置 Git 功能的必备工具，它具有实时功能，例如团队成员的在线状态和即时合并冲突检测。

借助 GitLive 添加到您的 IDE 的工具窗口，您可以查看团队中的哪些人在线、他们正在处理的问题和分支以及他们对这些分支所做的更改。

即时合并冲突检测是一个令人印象深刻的功能，它可以帮助您在合并冲突发生之前解决它们！ 编辑器中的指示器显示您的更改与其他人的更改之间的差异。 这些会在您和您的队友进行编辑时实时更新，并提供潜在合并冲突的早期警告。 所有开发团队的真正必备品！

数据库导航器

它为 PhpStorm 添加了广泛的数据库开发和维护功能。 除了合格且符合 IDE 的 SQL 和 PL/SQL 编辑器外，它还提供高级数据库连接管理、脚本执行支持、数据库对象浏览、数据和代码编辑器、支持数据库编译器操作、方法执行和调试、数据库对象工厂、以及其所有组件之间的各种导航功能。

我非常喜欢 DocBlocks，因为它们确保我们编写干净和高质量的代码。 这个有用的插件扩展了 PhpStorm 以支持 DocBlocks 中的注释并提供额外的属性功能，例如：类导入注释器、DocTags 中的类常量、注释类的自动别名导入、文档标签和属性名称的 Goto 等等。

如果你正在使用它，这个插件是绝对必备的。 它为 PhpStorm（和其他 JetBrains IDE）提供了完整的 Symfony 框架和组件支持。 它包含了 Symfony 开发所需的所有工具，包括模板、依赖注入、表单、路由等。

有什么反馈或建议吗？ 继续，在评论中告诉我！



原文:

If you are a PhpStorm user you know that it comes with many great features that make programming in PHP faster and easier. On top of that JetBrains marketplace offers a lot of useful plugins that aim at increasing our productivity and comfort of work. I decided to test some of them and here is my choice of tools that really make a difference!

Php Inspections ​(EA Extended)​

This plugin is an open-source Code Review and Static Code Analysis tool for PHP (aka inspections in JetBrains IDEs) and it seems to work better than native inspections. It’s a must-have add-on to PhpStorm to ensure high-quality coding. The default configuration is quite strict, but thanks to this you can avoid a lot of code smells (or disable some rules you don’t like).

It covers among other things: architecture-related issues, weak types control and possible code construct simplifications, performance issues, non-optimal, duplicate and suspicious "if" conditions, validation of magic methods usage and much more!

GitLive

This plugin is an absolute must to enhance PhpStorms’s built-in Git functionality with real-time features such as online presence for team members and instant merge conflict detection.

You can see who in your team is online, what issues and branches they are working on and the changes they’ve made on those branches, thanks to the tool window added by GitLive to your IDE.

Instant merge conflict detection is an impressive feature, that helps you resolve merge conflicts before they even happen! Indicators in the gutter of your editor show the difference between your changes and the changes of others. These update in real-time as you and your teammates are editing and provide an early warning of potential merge conflicts. A real must-have for all dev teams out there!

Database Navigator

Database Navigator is a database development, scripting and navigation tool. It adds extensive database development and maintenance capabilities to PhpStorm. Along with a qualified and IDE-compliant SQL and PL/SQL editor, it provides advanced database connection management, script execution support, database objects browsing, data and code editor, support for database compiler operations, method execution and debugging, database objects factory, as well as various navigation capabilities between all its components.

PhP Annotations

I am all about DocBlocks because they ensure that we write clean and quality code. This useful plugin extends PhpStorm to support annotations in DocBlocks and provides additional Attributes features like: Class import annotator, Class constants in DocTags, Auto alias import for annotation class, Goto for doc tags and property names, and many more.

Symfony Support

Symfony is an open-source PHP framework for web development that needs no introduction. If you’re using it, this plugin is an absolute must-have. It provides a full Symfony framework and component support for PhpStorm (and other JetBrains IDEs). It contains all the tools you need for Symfony development including templates, dependency injection, forms, routing, and more.

Have some feedback or suggestions? Go ahead and let me know in the comments!
item.author: hugomartinezzz

item.title: Javascript 最佳实践🔥 | Javascript best practices🔥
item.description:
1. 在 javascript 中声明和初始化数组
  2. 在javascript中找出总和，最小值和最大值
  3. 在javascript中对字符串、数字或对象的数组进行排序
  4. 在javascript中删除重复的数组值
  5. 在 javascript 中创建计数器对象或映射
  6. 在javascript中随机播放数组
  7. 在javascript中将十进制转换为二进制或十六进制

我们可以使用默认值（如“”、null 或 0）初始化特定大小的数组。您可能已经将这些用于一维数组，但是如何初始化二维数组/矩阵呢？

 const array = Array(5).fill(''); // Output (5) ["", "", "", "", ""] const matrix = Array(5).fill(0).map(()=>Array(5).fill(0)); // Output (5) [Array(5), Array(5), Array(5), Array(5), Array(5)] 0: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0] 1: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0] 2: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0] 3: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0] 4: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0] length: 5

了解有关文章主要来源的更多提示。

一旦暂停，devsimc 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devsimc 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devsimc 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devsimc 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

  1. Declare and Initialize Arrays in javascript
  2. Find out the sum, minimum and maximum value in javascript
  3. Sorting Array of String, Numbers or Objects in javascript
  4. Remove Duplicates array values in javascript
  5. Create a Counter Object or Map in javascript
  6. Ternary Operator in javascript
  7. Arrow Functions in javascript
  8. Shuffle an Array in javascript
  9. Rest & Spread operators in javascript
  10. Convert Decimal to Binary or Hexa in javascript

We can initialize array of particular size with default values like "", null or 0. You might have used these for the 1-D array but how about initializing 2-D array/matrix?

const array = Array(5).fill(''); 
// Output 
(5) ["", "", "", "", ""]

const matrix = Array(5).fill(0).map(()=>Array(5).fill(0)); 
// Output
(5) [Array(5), Array(5), Array(5), Array(5), Array(5)]
0: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
1: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
2: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
3: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
4: (5) [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
length: 5

2. Find out the sum, minimum and maximum value in javascript 

const array  = [5,4,7,8,9,2]; 
Sum in array javascript
array.reduce((a,b) => a+b);

// Output: 35
MAX in array javascript
array.reduce((a,b) => a>b?a:b);
// Output: 9

MIN in array javascript
array.reduce((a,b) => a<b?a:b);
// Output: 2

3. Sorting Array of String, Numbers or Objects in javascript 

const stringArr = ["Joe", "Kapil", "Steve", "Musk"]
stringArr.sort();
// Output
(4) ["Joe", "Kapil", "Musk", "Steve"]

stringArr.reverse();
// Output
(4) ["Steve", "Musk", "Kapil", "Joe"]

### Sort Number Array in javascript
const array  = [40, 100, 1, 5, 25, 10];
array.sort((a,b) => a-b);
// Output
(6) [1, 5, 10, 25, 40, 100]

array.sort((a,b) => b-a);
// Output
(6) [100, 40, 25, 10, 5, 1]

4. Remove Duplicates array values in javascript 

const array  = [5,4,7,8,9,2,7,5];
array.filter((item,idx,arr) => arr.indexOf(item) === idx);
// or
const nonUnique = [...new Set(array)];
// Output: [5, 4, 7, 8, 9, 2]

5. Create a Counter Object or Map in javascript 

let string = 'kapilalipak';
const table={}; 
for(let char of string) {
  table[char]=table[char]+1 || 1;
}
// Output
{k: 2, a: 3, p: 2, i: 2, l: 2}

6. Ternary Operator in javascript 

function Fever(temp) {
    return temp > 97 ? 'Visit Doctor!'
      : temp < 97 ? 'Go Out and Play!!'
      : temp === 97 ? 'Take Some Rest!';
}

// Output
Fever(97): "Take Some Rest!" 
Fever(100): "Visit Doctor!"

Find Out More Tips on main source of an article.

Luc van Kerkvoort - Jul 14

Once suspended, devsimc will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devsimc will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devsimc will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devsimc is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: devsimc

item.title: 3 个简短的 7 月前端提示 | 3 Short July Frontend tips
item.description:
嘿伙计！

我想谈谈改进显示密码按钮，为什么数字大的键盘比小数字的键盘好，通过←和→切换图片。

但在开始阅读之前，我在我的 Substack 时事通讯上留下了关于 UX 和 a11y 技巧的链接。 你知道要做什么😎

我们走吧！

使用焦点移动改进显示密码按钮

你有没有想过你是如何使用显示密码功能的？ 例如，尝试在 Twitter 上使用它。

单击按钮后，您必须手动单击输入字段，然后才能继续输入...😐

如果点击后焦点会自动移动到输入字段并且您继续输入而无需点击怎么办？ 这是在 ASOS 上完成的。 一探究竟！ 使用大数字而不是小数字的键盘

朋友们，现在，我正在 Booking 应用程序中填写我的生日日期。

当我在文本字段上录音时，我看到了带有字母和小数字的键盘。 当然，我输入 8 而不是 9😒 失败了，我想“Blimey...需要修复”。 你有多少次像我一样思考？ 这可以通过 inputmode HTML 属性来解决！ 数值有助于在键盘上显示易于敲击的数字。 干杯! 通过←和→切换图片

伙计们，我谴责不允许我使用键盘的应用程序😤尤其是当我想通过←或→切换图片时。 典型的例子是 iHerb 网络应用程序。

如果我们在产品页面上打开一个图库并按下 → 键查看下一张图片，应用程序将忽略我们。 是的，为什么允许使用键盘...🤦‍♂️

好的，让我们检查一下预订，您只需使用 ← 或 → 键即可查看公寓图片，而无需单击。 干杯! 附言

如果您有任何问题，请使用我的电子邮件进行交流👉 melnik909@ya.ru 。 免费

💪 Discover more free things from me 💪 从我这里发现更多免费的东西

PSS PSS
❤ Thank you so much, my sponsors: Ben Rinehart, Sergio Kagiema, Jesse Willard, Tanya Ten, Konstantinos Kapenekakis. ❤ 非常感谢，我的赞助商：Ben Rinehart、Sergio Kagiema、Jesse Willard、Tanya Ten、Konstantinos Kapenekakis。



原文:

Hey folks!

I'd like to talk about improving the Show password button, why a keyboard with big numbers is better than with small, switching pictures via ← and →.

But before embarking on reading I leave the link on my Substack newsletter about UX and a11y tips. You know what to make 😎

Let's go!

Improve the Show password button using focus moving

Did you think how you use show the password feature? As an example try to use it on Twitter.

After click on the button you have to click to the input field by hand and only then continue typing... 😐

And what if after click focus will move to the input field automatically and you continue typing without clicks? That was done on ASOS. Check it out! 😉

Use a keyboard with big numbers instead of small

Friends, now, I was filling in my birthday date in the Booking app.

When I taped on the text field I saw the keyboard with letters and small numbers. Sure, I failed by entering 8 instead of 9😒 and thought "Blimey... Need to fix". How often did you think like I? 😁

And that just can be solved by the inputmode HTML attribute! The numeric value helps to display the keyboard with numbers by which easy to hit. Cheers! 🥳

Switch pictures via ← and →

Folks, I condemn apps that don't allow me to use a keyboard 😤 Especially, when I want to switch pictures via ← or →. The typical example is the iHerb web app.

If we open a gallery on the product page and push the → key to look at the next picture the app will ignore us. Yep, why to allow using a keyboard...🤦‍♂️

Okay, let's check Booking where you can look at apartments' pictures without clicks using just the ← or → keys. Cheers! 🥳

P.S.

✉️ I love to help people learn more about user-friendly UI. If you have any questions, please, use my email for communication 👉 melnik909@ya.ru. It's free

💪 Discover more free things from me

P.S.S
❤ Thank you so much, my sponsors: Ben Rinehart, Sergio Kagiema, Jesse Willard, Tanya Ten, Konstantinos Kapenekakis.
item.author: melnik909

item.title: 从单个 Next.js 和 Umbraco 实例发芽无穷无尽的站点 | Sprout endless sites from a single Next.js and Umbraco instance
item.description:
构建普通的、单一的网站对你来说是否变得单调？ 你需要放松一下，尝试一些新的东西吗？ 好吧，你很幸运，我们已经为你的开发者生活增添了趣味。

在本文中，我们将向您展示如何使用单个 Umbraco 实例作为 CMS 并使用单个 Next.js 作为前端来构建多个不同的网站。 为了获得那种甜美的性能，我们还将使用 Enterspeed 作为速度层来提供我们的内容。

你们中的一些人可能会面带疑惑地坐着，想：“到底为什么要这样做？这仅仅是像使用Google 表格作为 CMS一样的疯狂想法吗？”。

尽管我承认我们喜欢在 Enterspeed 挑战极限并尝试一些疯狂的事情，但实际上这种场景有几个用例。 这些场景之一是微型网站。 如果使用得当，微型网站可以为您的企业提供极其强大的营销策略。

微型网站的力量🐜

首先，什么是微网站？

微型网站是与主网站分开存在的小型网站，通常位于其自己的域或子域中。 与主要网站不同，它通常只有一个目标，例如，推广单一产品、服务或活动。

即使它们有相似之处，微型网站也不是登录页面，因为登录页面只是一个页面。 着陆页也主要关注硬转化，而微型网站则更多地关注体验，这有助于帮助以后的转化。

让我们看几个例子。

假设我们是一家拥有多个子品牌或产品线的企业。 这些品牌/产品中的每一个都可能有自己独特的设计，也可能针对不同的人群。 例如，这可能是一家同时销售酒精和非酒精啤酒的啤酒厂。

拥有一个独特的微型网站，他们可以在其中完全控制视觉识别和交流，不仅会使信息更强大 - 在推动付费流量时，它还有助于提高广告相关性。

另一个类似的例子可能是一个品牌，其单一产品针对不同的人群——这可能是一种营养补充剂。

这种营养补充剂的目标群体可能是：

  • 希望降低骨质疏松症等疾病风险的老年人
  • 有兴趣获得额外表现的运动员
  • 关注健康生活方式的成年人

仅举几个。 您向这三个群体推销产品的方式差异很大——无论是在沟通方面还是在设计方面。

第三个例子可能是具有极其漫长而复杂的买家旅程的产品或服务——例如预制房屋。

一个家庭寻找房子从构思（顶部漏斗）到实际购买（底部漏斗）所需的时间可能是字面上的几年。

整个销售漏斗可以包含数百个接触点，并包含几个问题，例如：

  • 还有很多很多很多...

拥有战略性微型网站，在整个买家的旅程中提供帮助，这可能是他们宝贵的财富。

最后一个例子，在进入细节之前。 假设您是一家害虫防治公司。 您希望增加您在搜索引擎中的有机存在，以赢得更多客户并减少您对 SEM（搜索引擎中的付费广告）的依赖。

这里的策略可能是为每种类型的室内害虫建立一个微型站点，例如苍蝇、臭虫、黄蜂、老鼠、白蚁等。

这些网站中的每一个都与该特定主题高度相关 - 并且可以更容易地建立链接（一些网站所有者更喜欢链接到主题网站而不是公司网站）。

唷，那是很多营销话题。 引用 Linus Torvalds 的话： “谈话很便宜。 给我看看密码。”

设置您的项目

这个例子将围绕我们虚构的公司 - Enterbrew 构建。

开发啤酒

  • 啤酒
  • 洛雷姆啤酒
  • 冰啤酒

    • 这些微型网站中的每一个都有一个主页、一个列出不同啤酒的页面、每种啤酒的单独产品页面，最后是一个关于页面。

    然而，即使我们要从同一个后端和同一个前端运行所有这些，我们也不限于这种结构。 如果我们愿意，我们可以为每个页面使用不同的页面类型。

    让我们从设置一个 Umbraco 实例开始。

    设置 Umbraco

    首先下载并安装 Umbraco，例如在您的本地机器上（如果您愿意，也可以使用Umbraco Cloud ）。

    注意：我们目前正在为版本 10 开发 Enterspeed 集成，目前我们仅支持 V7、V8 和 V9。

    之后，进入 Umbraco 后台，点击Settings选择Document Types 。 在此示例中，我们使用 4 种文档类型：

    • 啤酒 - 我们所有啤酒的父页面
    • 啤酒——单品页面
    • 内容页面 - 用于一般内容 - 这里是我们的关于页面

    我们不会介绍每个模板的所有属性，因为它们可以是您想要的任何东西。 重要的是您将一种文档类型（在我们的示例中为 Home）设置为您的根节点。 这是在模板中的Permissions下完成的，您在其中将Allow as root设置为true 。

    接下来，转到Content并使用您刚刚创建的根节点类型创建一个新项目，在我们的示例中为 Home。 创建完成后，右键单击它并选择Culture and Hostnames 。 添加您希望用于此站点的域，例如https://www.devbeer.com 。

    对您要创建的所有网站重复上述步骤。

    现在是设置 Enterspeed 集成的时候了。 一旦我们将 Umbraco 站点与我们的 Enterspeed 帐户连接起来，我们所有的内容都将自动同步到 Enterspeed，从而为我们提供极快的速度层，并从本质上解耦我们的 Umbraco 实例。

    但在我们开始将内容爆破到 Enterspeed 之前，我们首先需要在 Enterspeed 内部创建一个数据源，它为我们提供了一个 API 密钥。

    转到 Enterspeed，单击Settings并选择Data sources 。 单击创建组并为您的组指定名称和类型。 接下来，添加一个数据源（我们的 Umbraco 实例）并选择一个环境，例如Development 。 单击添加，然后单击创建。

    太棒了，现在我们得到了一个 API 密钥。 让我们回到我们的 Umbraco 实例。

    首先通过NuGet添加 Enterspeed 集成。 我们目前有V7 、 V8和V9版本的集成。

    安装集成后，您将在Settings下看到一个名为Enterspeed Settings的新选项卡。 添加https://api.enterspeed.com作为 Enterspeed 端点和您刚刚在 API key 下创建的API 密钥。

    如果一切顺利，请测试您的连接并单击保存配置。

    现在，当您保存和发布时，所有内容都将同步到您的 Enterspeed 帐户。 如果您需要播种已发布的内容，请单击内容并选择Enterspeed 内容选项卡。 选择种子并单击种子按钮。

    太棒了——现在是设置 Enterspeed 的时候了。

    设置 Enterspeed

    通过转到Home下的Source 实体，验证您的所有内容是否已同步。 选择您的数据源并检查您的所有内容是否都存在。 此外，确认您在 Umbraco 的文化和主机名下输入的域与 URL 列中的域匹配。

    如果一切都很好，那么是时候创建我们的第一个模式了。 那么首先，什么是模式，为什么我们需要它们？

    这是通过创建模式来完成的。

    模式使组合多个数据源变得容易，并使深度嵌套的属性更易于访问。

    对于我们的示例，我们将为我们在 Umbraco 中创建的每种文档类型创建一个模式，并为导航创建两个模式（一个用于单个导航项，一个包含所有导航项）。

    一旦我们创建并部署了模式，将为每个匹配模式触发器的源实体生成一个视图。

    它由一个源组（您的数据源）组成，并包含一个或多个源实体类型（内容类型）的数组。

    在触发器属性中，我们首先定义源组（这里是我们的 Umbraco 数据源组），然后定义源实体类型（例如我们的页面类型）。

    此外，我们还需要定义从前端调用时如何访问这些生成的视图。 这里我们有两个选项：句柄和 URL。

    所有基于 Umbraco 文档类型的模式都反映了一个实际的页面，因此我们将使用 URL 路由选项。

    另一方面，导航是一个将在整个应用程序中使用的实体，因此我们将使用 Handle route 选项。

    让我们看一下每个模式。

    啤酒模式

    此模式从Beer源实体类型获取其内容，并且可通过 URL 进行路由。 我们还设置了一些操作。 这里的动作将触发另一个模式的过程。 由于我们的Beer是Beers的子级，它将显示所有子级 ( Beer ) 的列表，因此我们需要一种方法来在其子级 ( Beer ) 更改时更新视图。 这是通过动作类型过程完成的。

    在流程操作中，我们还需要提供一个originId ，它是在摄取时给出的源实体的 ID（在 Umbraco 中它反映了页面 ID）。 我们希望这个originId与我们当前源实体的(Beer) originParentId 相同（也在摄取时给出，这里反映了页面的父级 - Beers ）。

    所以总结一下动作。 每当我们的一个Beer视图发生变化时，它都会触发对其父视图的处理，这里是Beers视图。

    下面我们有properties ，这是实际的内容。 在这里，我们定义了一个类型，我们将用于在前端渲染逻辑和页面的 URL，我们将使用它来链接到概览页面上的各个啤酒。 最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们通过使用动态映射从 Umbraco 获取该页面的所有内容。 我们将所有这些分组到内容下，并使用以下方法将它们全部映射出来： “*”：“p”

    啤酒模式

    就像上面的模式一样，我们已经定义了触发器，将路由设置为 URL，并将beers指定为属性下的类型。

    在content下，突然发生了很多事情，虽然一开始看起来很复杂，但当我们一步一步看它时，其实很简单。

    我们之前一直在映射字符串，现在我们想要输出一个数组。 那么让我们看一下结构。

    首先，我们将类型定义为数组。 您不必对字符串执行此操作的原因只是语法糖，因为字符串是最常用的属性类型。

    接下来是输入属性。 这是您希望从中检索集合的位置。 我们使用$lookup类型来定义源实体必须匹配的类似查询的条件。

    我们想要的运算符是 equal 运算符，我们想要查看的sourceEntityProperty是originParentId 。 这应该匹配 originId的值（这里是Beers originId ）。

    换句话说。 我们希望采购以Beers originId作为其OriginParentId的实体。 比尔斯的所有孩子。

    凉爽的。 现在是最后一部分 - 内容。 我们在这里使用的类型是引用类型，它用于引用另一个模式——在本例中是我们之前创建的Beer模式。 我们使用模式的别名引用另一个模式——它恰好也是beer 。

    最后，我们需要指定源实体的 ID，它位于id下，并且由于我们正在“内部”其中一个项目中查找它，因此我们在它前面加上item ，然后变成： item.id

    瞧。 我们现在有一系列我们所有的啤酒🍻

    家庭模式

    现在用一个简单的模式放松一下。 我们抓取home源实体类型，通过 URL 使其可路由，将类型设置为home ，并动态映射 content 下的所有内容。

    页面模式

    此模式的结构与 Home 模式相同。

    导航项模式

    现在是时候创建我们单独的导航项了。 首先，我们定义我们希望导航包含哪些源实体类型。 在我们的示例中，我们只需要一个非常简单的导航，仅包含父页面（意味着不是单个啤酒）。 我们也不希望它包含我们的主页，因为我们将使用该徽标导航回它。

    与我们之前的模式不同，我们不包含路由，因为此模式仅作为导航模式的一部分工作，并且永远不会从前端调用。

    在操作下，我们执行与Beer模式中相同的操作。 我们告诉它在源实体类型page或beers的视图发生新情况时处理其父级。 在这种情况下，父节点是站点节点home 。

    对于属性，我们获取label的metaData.name 、我们为其他模式提供的类型以及href的url 。 从 Umbraco 中，我们还获得了一个 sortOrder，我们可以使用它来对前端的导航进行排序。

    导航架构

    就像装饰东方地毯一样，是时候将它们绑在一起了。 我们使用根节点 home 作为源实体类型。

    在路由中，我们将使用句柄，因为导航是一个将在整个网站中获取的组件，而不仅仅是一个页面。

    然而，由于我们正在构建一个多站点解决方案，我们需要一种方法来区分每个站点的导航。 我们通过使用-{url}附加句柄navigation来做到这一点，它会插入 home 的 URL，它是根 URL。

    在properties内部，我们创建了一个数组，就像我们的Beers模式一样，它使用对另一个模式的引用，这里是navigationItem 。

    那是我们需要创建的最后一个模式。 现在我们只需要部署我们的模式，就可以生成我们全新的视图。

    部署模式后，您可以导航到Home下的Generated views以查看结果。

    创建环境客户端

    我们需要做的最后一件事是创建一个或多个环境客户端。

    你问。

    通常每个网站都有一个环境客户端，同样建议每个环境都有一个环境客户端。

    但是，我们正在构建一个基于单个 Next.js 实例的多站点，这使它有点不同。

    例如，如果您想使用 Enterspeeds Routes API来构建站点地图，那么您需要单独的环境客户端。 如果没有，那么您只需使用一个环境客户端即可。 所以，选择权在你。

    不用担心，稍后我们将向您展示如何支持多个环境客户端。

    要创建环境客户端，请转到Settings并选择Environment settings 。

    如果您已正确完成所有操作，那么您应该已经在“域”部分中看到了您在 Umbraco 中的Culture 和 Hostnames下输入的所有域的列表。 如果没有，那么您需要手动创建它们及其主机名。 但是现在，让我们假设你已经正确地完成了所有事情 - 做得好🎉

    转到环境客户端并单击创建。 为您的环境客户端命名并选择一个环境。 然后单击创建。 接下来，选择要附加到此环境客户端的所有域。 如果您打算只使用一个，则将它们全部选中。

    注意：如果以后添加更多站点，需要去编辑环境客户端并添加这些域。

    现在我们已经准备好进入前端——我们的 Next.js 实例。 在这里，我们还会遇到一种非常有名的红色甲壳类动物，不幸的是它不是 Zoidberg 。

    设置 Next.js

    现在是时候设置我们的前端了。 为此，我们将使用 Next.js、Express 和秘诀，这使得只使用单个 Next.js 实例Krabs成为可能。

    什么是螃蟹？ 作者将其描述为： “......一个企业级 Express.js/Fastify 中间件，用于从单个 Next.js 实例为数千个不同的网站提供服务。”

    非常酷 - 并且有一个很棒的名字。

    向Michele Riva大喊这个超棒的套餐👏

    注意： Krabs 强制您使用自定义服务器。 因此，不支持部署到 Vercel。 首先要做的事情是从创建您的 Next 应用程序开始。 接下来，安装Krabs和Express 。

    注意： Krabs 配置的步骤是从他们的文档中借来的。

    创建一个名为 server.js 的新文件。 这将是我们的自定义 Express.js 服务器的入口点，它处理我们的 Next.js 实例。

    接下来，在项目的根文件夹中创建一个名为 .krabs.js（或 .krabs.config.js）的文件。 我们还通过添加一个名为enterspeedDomain的属性来扩展标准配置。 要真正看到我们在做什么，我们需要配置我们的hosts 文件。 插入您要在本地环境中查看的所有域名。

    太棒了，现在为每个网站创建“家”。 在您的pages目录中，为您的每个网站创建一个与 Krabs 配置文件中提供的名称相匹配的文件夹。

    注意：_app 和 _document 页面对每个网站都是通用的。

    要测试一切是否正常，您可以为每个网站创建一个带有简单“hello world”功能的 index.js 文件。

    要查看结果运行： node server.js

    如果一切正常，您现在应该能够使用您在主机文件和 Krabs 配置中输入的 URL 查看每个网站。

    接下来，让我们创建与 Enterspeed 的连接。 创建一个名为 enterspeed.js 的文件

    对于每种路由类型，我们都有一个调用函数和一个函数： getByHandle和getByUrl 。 您会注意到我们在getByUrl函数中使用meta扩展了响应，这样我们就可以正确处理 404 错误。 每个函数都使用句柄​​/url 和“租户”（Krabs 中配置的网站名称）调用我们的调用函数。

    我们包含“租户”的原因是我们可以区分我们的网站并为每个网站使用不同的环境客户端 - 如果我们选择的话。 在我们的示例中，我们只是使用了相同的方法。

    接下来，在pages目录中创建一个_app.js文件。 在这里，我们将获取租户名称（网站名称）、 enterspeedDomain ，并获取我们的导航。

    在我们的App.getInitialProps 中，您会看到我们通过 getByHandle 获取导航并将${enterspeedDomain}添加到名称中，这与我们在 Enterspeed 中的导航模式中创建的路线名称相匹配。

    然后将租户和enterspeedDomain添加到pageProps中，该页面与导航一起返回。 这些现在可以在我们的App功能中使用。

    在我们的App函数中，您会看到我们扩展了租户对象以包含一个名为tenantDetails的东西。 这只是我们添加的一些额外布局配置，也可以在 Umbraco 中完成。 这只是为了展示前端开发人员在无法访问 Umbraco 实例的情况下如何做到这一点。

    我们选择将其添加到单独的文件中，而不是 Krabs 配置中，以免将特定布局混入 Krabs 配置中。

    最后，我们用一个名为MainLayout的组件包装 App 组件，在其中我们将租户和导航作为道具传递。 让我们来看看那个组件。

    我们基于出色的 UI 框架 - Chakra UI制作了一个简单的布局，但这是简洁的部分。 我们可以为我们的每个站点使用完全不同的 UI 框架/库 - 而不会损害主要性能。

    是的，你没看错。 谈论为开发人员带来力量和灵活性。

    开发人员之间不再有关于使用哪个 UI 框架/库的内部争斗。 不再试图争论 Tailwind CSS 是否是纯粹的邪恶。 就像旧的汉堡王广告一样，每个人都按照自己的方式去做。

    您现在可以选择最适合用例的 UI 框架/库。

    我们可以通过使用 Next.js 的Dynamic Import来实现这一点。 在我们的_app.js文件中，我们可以根据租户名称（网站名称）为每个网站使用唯一的组件，而不是使用MainLayout组件。 这可能看起来像这样：

    现在，让我们看看如何设置页面。 我们将使用 Next.js 的 Dynamic Routes - 更具体地说是他们的Optional catch all routes 。 这将允许我们捕获所有路线并将它们与 Enterspeed 中的相应视图相匹配。

    在您的每个网站文件夹中，创建一个名为 [[...slug]].js 的文件

    在我们的getServerSideProps函数中，我们挂钩到它的上下文并获取租户、域和slug 。 我们将这三个变量传递给我们在enterspeed.js文件中编写的getByUrl函数，我们将其设置为数据变量。

    然后我们检查data.status是否等于404 ，如果是，我们将响应对象中的statusCode更改为 404 并将我们的notFound变量设置为 true。

    然后将数据和notFound作为道具返回。

    在我们的Content组件中，我们检查notFound是否为 true，如果是，我们使用 Next.js 的Error 组件，如果不是，我们返回我们的自定义Entity组件。

    我们的 Entity 组件负责根据我们之前在模式中定义的类型呈现正确的组件。

    您可能会想： “嗯，但是如果我只想为一个特定页面自定义布局怎么办？我是否应该创建一个具有自己独特类型的全新架构？”

    没有。 预定义路由始终优先于动态路由。

    这意味着如果您想为您的联系人页面设置一个独特的布局，您只需创建一个名为“contact.js”的页面，它现在将响应 domain.com/contact。

    好吧，总结一下，让我们看看我们将如何使用我们的数据来显示我们的啤酒列表。

    所有的数据都已经传递给 view 属性，所以剩下的就是映射我们的啤酒数组并将它们呈现在一个漂亮的网格视图中，如下所示：

    基于 Chakra UI 的代码可能如下所示：

    而已。 我们希望您喜欢这篇文章，它为如何使用微型网站提供了一些灵感。

    干杯🍻



    原文:

    Has building ordinary, single websites become monotonous for you? Do you need to let loose and just try something new for a bit? Well, you're in luck, we've got just the thing to spice up your developer life.

    In this article, we will show you how to build multiple different websites using just a single Umbraco instance as the CMS and a single Next.js as the frontend. And to get that sweet, sweet performance, we're also going to use Enterspeed as a speed layer to serve our content.

    Some of you might sit with a puzzled look on your face, thinking: "Why on earth would one do that? Is this merely an insane idea like using Google Sheets as a CMS?".

    Even though I'll admit that we love to push the envelope here at Enterspeed and try out crazy things, there are actually several use cases for this scenario. One of these scenarios is microsites. Microsites can, if used correctly, be an extremely powerful marketing strategy for your business.

    Pssst... Don't care about marketing and strategy but simply want the geeky stuff? 🤓 Then you can safely skip the section below.

    The power of Microsites 🐜

    First of all, what the dickens is a microsite?

    A microsite is a small website that exists separate from the primary website, often on its own domain or subdomain. Unlike the primary website, it often has just a single goal, for instance, to promote a single product, service, or event.

    Even though they share similarities, a microsite is not a landing page since a landing page is just a single page. A landing page is also focused mainly on hard conversions, whereas a microsite focus can be more about an experience, which can help to assist conversions later down the road.

    Let's look at a few examples.

    Say we are a business that owns several sub-brands or product lines. Each of these brands/products may have its own unique design and may also be targeting different demographics. This could for instance be a brewery selling both alcoholic and non-alcoholic beers.

    Having a unique microsite, where they can fully control the visual identity and the communication, would not only make the message more powerful - it would also help with ad relevance when driving paid traffic to it.

    Another similar example could be a brand with a single product that targets different demographics - this could be a nutrient supplement.

    The target groups for this nutrient supplement could be:

    • Older people who wish to reduce the risk of diseases like osteoporosis
    • Athletes who are interested in gaining the extra performance
    • Health-focused adults who are all about a healthy lifestyle

    Just to name a few. How you market a product to these three groups differs highly - both in communication and design.

    A third example could be a product or a service with an extremely long and complicated buyer's journey - for instance prefabricated houses.

    The time it takes for a family looking for a house to go from idea (top-funnel) to actual purchase (bottom-funnel) can be literal years.

    This whole sales funnel can contain hundreds of touchpoints and consist of several questions like:

    • "Rent or buy?"
    • "How much money for a down payment?"
    • "New house vs. old house"
    • And much, much, much more...

    Having strategic microsites, which assist along the whole buyer's journey can be their bytes worth in gold.

    Last example, before jumping into the nitty-gritty details. Say you are a pest control company. You want to increase your organic presence in the search engines to gain more customers and reduce your reliance on SEM (paid advertisement in search engines).

    A strategy here could be to build a microsite for each type of house pest, e.g. flies, bed bugs, wasps, rats, termites, etc.

    Each of these sites will be highly relevant for this specific topic - and can be easier to build links to (some website owners prefer to link to a topic website instead of a company website).

    Phew, that was a lot of marketing talk. To quote Linus Torvalds: “Talk is cheap. Show me the code.”

    Setting up your project

    This example will be built around our fictional company - Enterbrew.

    Enterbrew is a brewery consisting of 4 subbrands targeting different demographics.

    • Dev Beer
    • ToBeer
    • Lorem Beer
    • Ice beer

    Each of these microsites will have a homepage, a page listing the different beers, individual product pages for each beer, and finally an about page.

    However, even though we're going to run all of this from the same backend and the same frontend, we're not limited to this structure. If we wanted, we could use different page types for each page.

    Let's start by setting up a Umbraco instance.

    Setting up Umbraco

    Start by downloading and installing Umbraco, e.g. on your local machine (You can also use Umbraco Cloud if you prefer).

    Note: We're currently working on an Enterspeed-integration for version 10, for now, we're only supporting V7, V8, and V9.

    Afterward, go to the Umbraco Backoffice, click on Settings select Document Types. In this example we're using 4 document types:

    • Home - the homepage which will also be our root node
    • Beers - the parent page of all our beers
    • Beer - the single product page
    • Content page - used for general content - here our about page

    We're not going to go over all the properties of each template, since they can be whatever you want. The important thing is that you set one of your document types, in our example, Home, as your root node. This is done under Permissions in the template, where you set the Allow as root to true.

    Next, go to Content and create a new item with the root node type, in our example, Home, you just created. Once it's been created, right-click on it and choose Culture and Hostnames. Add the domain you wish to use for this site, e.g. https://www.devbeer.com.

    Repeat the step above for all the sites you wish to create.

    Now it's time to set up the Enterspeed integration. Once we've connected our Umbraco site with our Enterspeed account, all of our content will be automatically synced to Enterspeed providing us with a blazing-fast speed layer, and essentially decoupling our Umbraco instance.

    But before we start blasting content into Enterspeed, we first need to create a data source inside Enterspeed, which provides us with an API key.

    Go to Enterspeed, click on Settings and select Data sources. Click Create Group and give your group a name and a type. Next, add a data source (our Umbraco instance) and select an environment, e.g. Development. Click Add and then click Create.

    Pssst... If you don't have an account already, you can create one right here 👉 https://app.enterspeed.com/signup (no credit card required).

    Awesome, now we got an API key. Let's head back to our Umbraco instance.

    Start by adding the Enterspeed integration via NuGet. We currently have integrations for versions V7, V8, and V9.

    Once you have installed the integration, you'll see a new tab under Settings called Enterspeed Settings. Add https://api.enterspeed.com as the Enterspeed endpoint and the API key you just created under API key.

    Test your connection and click Save configuration if everything goes well.

    All content will now be synced to your Enterspeed account when you save and publish. If you need to seed already published content then click on Content and select the Enterspeed Content tab. Choose Seed and click on the Seed-button.

    Excellent - now it's time to set up Enterspeed.

    Setting up Enterspeed

    Verify that all your content has been synced, by going to Source entities under Home. Choose your data source and check if all your content is present. Furthermore, confirm that the domain you entered under Culture and Hostnames in Umbraco matches the one in the URL column.

    If everything is good, then it's time to create our first schemas. So first, what is a schema, and why do we need them?

    One of the powerful things about Enterspeed is its ability to transform and model content into exactly the views you need. This is done by creating schemas.

    Schemas make it easy to combine multiple data sources, and to make deeply nested properties more accessible.

    For our example, we will create a schema for each document type we created in Umbraco, and two for the navigation (one for single navigation items and one containing all the navigation items).

    Once we have created and deployed the schemas, a view will be generated for each source entity that matches the schemas trigger.

    💡 A trigger defines what you want the schema to "trigger on". It consists of a source group (your data source) and contains an array of one or more soure entity types (types of content).

    In the trigger property, we first define the source group (here our Umbraco data source group), and next the source entity types (e.g. our page types).

    Furthermore, we also need to define how we want to access these generated views when called from the front end. Here we have two options: Handle and URL.

    All the schemas that are based on document types from Umbraco reflect an actual page, therefore we will use the URL route option.

    The navigation on the other hand is an entity that will be used across the entire app, therefore we will use the Handle route option.

    Let's take a look at each schema.

    Beer-schema

    This schema gets its content from the Beer source entity type and is routable via URL. The URL value will reflect the one in the source entity, which is identical to the page URL in Umbraco.

    We have also set up some actions. The actions here will trigger a process of another schema. Since our Beer is a child of Beers, which will show a list of all its children (Beer), we need a way to update the view whenever its children (Beer) change. This is done via the action type process.

    In the process action, we also need to provide an originId, which is the ID of the source entity given upon ingest (In Umbraco it reflects the page ID). We want this originId to be the same as our current source entities' (Beer) originParentId (also given upon ingest, here reflects the page's parent - Beers).

    So to sum up actions. Every time one of our Beer views changes, it triggers a processing of its parent, here the Beers view.

    Below we have properties, which is the actual content. Here we define a type, that we will use for rendering logic in our frontend and the URL to the page, which we will use to link to the individual beers on the overview page. Last, but not least, we grab all the content we have from Umbraco for this page, by using dynamic mapping. We group all of this under content and map it all out by using: "*": "p"

    Beers-schema

    Just like the schema above, we have defined triggers, set route to URL, and specified beers as the type under properties.

    Under content, there's suddenly a lot going on, and even though it might seem complex at first, it's actually quite simple when we go through it step by step.

    Where we before had been working mapping out strings, we now want to output an array. So let's look at the structure.

    First, we define the type as an array. The reason why you don't have to do this on strings is simply syntactic sugar since strings are the most commonly used property type.

    Next is the input property. This is where you wish to retrieve the collection from. We use the $lookup type to define a query-like criteria that the source entities have to match.

    The operator we want is the equal operator and the sourceEntityProperty we want to look at is originParentId. This should matchValue of originId (which here is the Beers originId).

    So in other words. We want to source entities that have Beers originId as their OriginParentId. All the children of Beers.

    Cool. Now the last part - the content. The items field is used to map the results.

    The type we are using here is a reference type, which is used to reference another schema - in this case the Beer schema we created before. We reference another schema using the schemas' alias - which happens to also be beer.

    Lastly, we need to specify the ID of the source entity, which is found under id, and since we're looking for it "inside" one of the items, we prepend it with item, which then becomes: item.id

    And voilà. We now have an array of all our beers 🍻

    Home-schema

    And now to relax with a simple schema. We grab the home source entity type, make it routable via URL, set the type to home, and dynamically map all the content under content.

    Page-schema

    This schema is structured the same way as the Home-schema.

    Navigation item-schema

    Now it's time to create our individual navigation items. First, we define which source entity types we want the navigation to consist of. In our example, we just want a very simple navigation, consisting of only parent pages (meaning not the individual beers). We also don't want it to include our homepage since we will use the logo for navigating back to it.

    Unlike our previous schemas, we don't include a route, since this schema only will work as part of our navigation schema, and never will be called from the front end.

    Under actions, we do the same as we did in the Beer schema. We tell it to process its parent whenever something new happens to views with source entity type page or beers. The parent in this case the site node, home.

    For properties, we grab the metaData.name for the label, the type we have given our other schemas, and the url for href. From Umbraco, we also get a sortOrder, which we can use to sort our navigation in the front end.

    Navigation schema

    Just like a decorative oriental rug, it's time to tie it all together. We use our root node, home, as our source entity type.

    In route, we will use handles, since the navigation is a component that will be fetched throughout the website, and not just a page.

    However, since we're building a multi-site solution, we need a way to differentiate the navigation for each site from the other. We do this by appending the handle, navigation, with -{url}, which inserts the URL for home, which is the root URL.

    Inside properties, we create an array that, just like our Beers schema, uses a reference to another schema, here navigationItem.

    That was the last schema we needed to create. Now we just need to deploy our schemas so our brand new views can be generated.

    Once you have deployed the schemas, you can navigate to Generated views under Home to see the result.

    Creating environment client(s)

    The last thing we need to do is to create one or more environment clients.

    "Well, what is it, one or more?" you ask.

    Normally you have one environment client per website and, as the same suggests, per environment.

    However, we are building a multisite based on a single Next.js instant, which makes it a little different.

    If you want to use Enterspeeds Routes API, for instance, to build a sitemap, then you need individual environment clients. If not, then you can do it with just a single environment client. So, the choice is yours.

    Don't worry, we will show you how you can support multiple environment clients later on.

    To create an environment client, go to Settings and select Environment settings.

    If you have done everything correctly, then you should already see a list in the Domains section of all the domains you have entered in Umbraco under Culture and Hostnames. If not, then you need to manually create them and their hostnames. But for now, let's assume you've done everything correctly - well done 🎉

    Go to Environment clients and click Create. Give your environment client a name and choose an environment. Then click Create. Next, choose all the domains you want to attach to this environment client. If you're planning on only using one, then select them all.

    Note: If you add more sites in the future, you need to go to edit the environment client and add these domains.

    Now that we're ready to move on to the front end - our Next.js instance. Here we will also meet a very famous, red crustacean, which unfortunately is not Zoidberg.

    Setting up Next.js

    Now it's time to set up our front end. For this, we're going to use Next.js, Express, and the secret sauce which makes it possible to use just a single Next.js instance, Krabs.

    What is Krabs? The author describes it as: "...an enterprise-ready Express.js/Fastify middleware for serving thousands of different websites from a single Next.js instance."

    Pretty freaking cool - and with an awesome name nonetheless.

    Shout out to Michele Riva for this awesome package 👏

    Note: Krabs forces you to use a custom server. Therefore, deployments to Vercel are not supported. Krabs is also only supporting SSR at the moment, and not SSG nor ISR.

    First things first - start by creating your Next app. Next, install Krabs and Express.

    Note: The steps for the Krabs configuration are borrowed from their documentation.

    Create a new file called server.js. This will be the entry point for our custom Express.js server, which handles our Next.js instance.

    Next, create a file called .krabs.js (or .krabs.config.js) inside your project's root folder. Krabs will automatically import the configuration from this file.

    We have extended the standard configuration by also adding a property called enterspeedDomain. The value for the enterspeedDomain should be the same as the one given in Enterspeed (and Umbraco).

    To actually see what we're doing, we need to configure our hosts file. Insert all the domain names you want to view in your local environment.

    Awesome, now to create the "home" for each website. Inside your pages directory, create a folder for each of your websites matching the name provided in the Krabs configuration file.

    Note: _app and _document pages are common to every website.

    To test out that everything is working, you can create an index.js file with a simple "hello world"-function for each of the websites.

    To see the result run: node server.js

    If everything is working correctly, you should now be able to view each website using the URL you entered in your hosts file and Krabs configuration.

    Next, let's create our connection to Enterspeed. Create a file called enterspeed.js

    We have a call function and a function for each route type: getByHandle and getByUrl. You will notice that we in the getByUrl function have extended the response with meta, that way we can correctly handle 404-errors. Each of the functions calls our call function with a handle/url and a "tenant" (the website name configured in Krabs).

    The reason why we include "tenant" is so we can differentiate between our websites and use a different environment client for each - if we choose. In our example, we have simply used the same.

    Next, create an _app.js file in the pages directory. Here we will get the tenant name (website name), the enterspeedDomain, and also fetch our navigation.

    In our App.getInitialProps you'll see we fetch the navigation via getByHandle and we add the ${enterspeedDomain} to the name, which matches the route name we created in our navigation schema in Enterspeed.

    The tenant and enterspeedDomain are then added to the pageProps, which gets returned alongside the navigation. These are now available in our App function.

    In our App function, you'll see we expand the tenant object to also include something called tenantDetails. This is simply some extra layout configuration we've added, which also could have been done in Umbraco. This is just to show how the front-end developer could do this if they didn't have access to the Umbraco instance.

    We've chosen to add it in a separate file and not the Krabs configuration, so as to not mix layout specific into the Krabs configuration.

    Lastly, we wrap the App Component with a component called MainLayout, where we pass tenant and navigation as props. Let's take a look at that component.

    We've made a simple layout, based on the awesome UI framework - Chakra UI, but here's the neat part. We could have used a completely different UI framework/library for each of our sites - without hurting the main performance.

    Yes, you read that correctly. Talking about bringing power and flexibility to the developer.

    No more internal battles between developers on which UI framework/library to use. No more trying to argue if Tailwind CSS is pure evil or not. Just like an old Burger King commercial, everybody gets it their way.

    You are now able to choose the UI framework/library that best fits the use case.

    We can achieve this by using Next.js' Dynamic Import. In our _app.js file, instead of using the MainLayout component, we could have used a unique component for each website, based on the tenant name (website name). This could look like this:

    Now, let's look at how we could set up the pages. We're going to use Next.js' Dynamic Routes - more specifically their Optional catch all routes. This will allow us to catch all routes and match them with the corresponding view in Enterspeed.

    Inside each of your website folders, create a file called [[...slug]].js

    In our getServerSideProps function, we hook into its context and grab the tenant, domain, and slug. We pass these three variables on to the getByUrl function we wrote in our enterspeed.js file, which we set as our data variable.

    We then check to see if data.status is equal to 404, if so we change the statusCode in our response object to 404 and set our notFound variable to true.

    The data and the notFound are then returned as props.

    In our Content component we check to see if notFound is true, if so we use Next.js' Error component, if not we return our custom Entity component.

    Our Entity component is responsible for rendering the correct components, based on the type we defined in our schemas earlier.

    You may be thinking: "Hmm, but what if I want to have a custom layout for just one specific page? Should I then create a whole new schema with its own unique type?"

    Nope. Next.js got your back. Predefined routes always take precedence over dynamic routes.

    This means if you want a unique layout for your Contact page, you simply create a page called "contact.js", and this will now respond to domain.com/contact.

    Well, to round things up, let's look at how we would use our data to display a list of our beers.

    All of the data has been passed to the view prop, so all that is left is to map over our array of beers and render them in a nice grid view, which will look like this:

    The code, which is based on Chakra UI, could look something like this:

    That's it. We hope you enjoyed the article and it provided some inspiration on how microsites can be used.

    Cheers 🍻
item.author: kaspera

item.title: 勇敢的小鸡骰子冒险 | Brave Chicky Dice Adventure
item.description:
一之濑贵根

发表于这只是一个简单的回合制游戏，你只需要掷骰子，击败敌人，然后尽可能地去最高楼层。

通过下达命令帮助 Chicky 爬上自然塔。 有 4 种可能的命令可以与保卫自然之塔的幽灵战斗。

通过掷骰子，您可以根据骰子上出现的数字使用可能的命令来引导小鸡。

  • 此外，您可以挑战您的朋友并吹嘘您的最高分。

    这些天我并没有放弃实践 Web 开发，而且我很难创建所有这些东西。

    这个游戏最好在横向模式下观看。

    资源

罗杰·维纳斯·爱尔康 - 克里斯蒂安·海尔曼—— 一旦暂停，takanechinose 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，takanechinose 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，takanechinose 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 takanechinose 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Takane Ichinose

Posted on Jul 14

This is just a simple turn-based game where you just have to throw a dice, defeat the enemy, and go to the highest floor as you can.

Help Chicky to climb up the Natural Tower by giving it a command. Chicky doesn't quite understand yet what it will do.

There are 4 possible commands to fight the ghost defending the Natural Tower.

By rolling the dice, you can guide Chicky with the possible commands depends on the number appeared on the dice.

  • == 1 Do nothing
  • >= 2 Defend
  • >= 3 Defend, Attack
  • >= 5 Defend, Attack, Heal

Also, you may challenge your friends and brag your highest score.

I haven't been ditching practicing web development these days, and I had hard time creating all these stuff.

This game is best viewed in landscape mode.

Resources

Roger Viñas Alcon - Jun 22

Christian Heilmann - Jul 1

Once suspended, takaneichinose will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, takaneichinose will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by takaneichinose will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If takaneichinose is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: takaneichinose

item.title: 测试 SSL 证书的方法 | Ways to test SSL Certificates
item.description:
皮尤什·马图

发表于下载 sslyze-4.1.1-exe.zip 并将 sslyze-4.1.0 文件夹解压到本地驱动器

  • 打开命令提示符并切换到提取 sslyze-3.0.8 的文件夹： o sslyze.exe --regular [主机名或 IP 地址] o sslyze.exe --regular ep.firstam.com

    一旦暂停，gpmattoo 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    • 一旦解除暂停，gpmattoo 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，gpmattoo 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 gpmattoo 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    Piyush Mattoo

    Posted on Jul 14

    1. Download sslyze-4.1.1-exe.zip and extract sslyze-4.1.0 folder to local drive

    2. Open command prompt and change to the folder where sslyze-3.0.8 was extracted: o sslyze.exe --regular [hostname or IP address] o sslyze.exe --regular ep.firstam.com

      o sslyze.exe --regular 172.17.22.117

    Once suspended, gpmattoo will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, gpmattoo will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by gpmattoo will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If gpmattoo is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: gpmattoo

item.title: 成为一名自学成才的开发人员好吗？ | Is it good to be a Self Taught Developer ?
item.description:
怎么了女孩/伙计们！ 在这里，我与另一篇文章继续自学开发者系列。

在本节中，我们将回答“自学成才的开发人员好吗？ ”。 我说“我们”是因为我希望你们/女孩参与讨论😊😉。

本文即将发表的观点只是我的观点和经验，仅此而已。 大家也可以在评论区分享你的看法。

为了得到像这样的任何问题的答案，我做的最好的事情就是列出该主题的优点和缺点。 所以让我们去做吧。

成为自学成才的开发人员的优势

是的！ 当你独自一人时，事情并不容易。 还在自学：

  • 给了我时间感。
  • 让我意识到纪律的重要性，因为这是你需要保持一致的。
  • 让我意识到一致性的重要性。
  • 感觉它永远不够，我的朋友促进了你的成长。（如果积极采取😅😅）
  • 最重要的是让我对我输入的所有内容和我所做的一切负责。

一致性可以让你比强度更熟练。

每天保持相同的强度。

成为自学成才的开发人员的缺点

即使它有这么多优点，它也有它自己的缺点：

  • 缺乏朋友。
  • 缺乏庞大的网络。
  • 缺少可以帮助您解决任何错误的女孩/男孩。

沟通

虽然这些缺点乍一看似乎并不严重，但由于这些小缺点，自学成才的开发人员缺乏沟通和合作的技能。

我认为沟通不应该是一个问题，任何人都可以做到，但不，它并不像看起来那么简单。

沟通是你工作中非常重要的一部分，因为

  • 这是让你找到工作的最重要的技能之一
  • 这会让其他人认为你对自己所说的话非常有信心，
  • 这会让你与众不同，因为不是每个人都有良好的沟通技巧。

该死的现在感觉缺点真的很大！

缺点的解决方案

永远不要害怕重塑自己

对于大多数自学成才的开发人员来说，即使是艰难的沟通也是一个巨大的问题，它可以逐渐得到改善。

没有什么可以学习或改进的。
很明显，一些最初的尝试可能会让你感到紧张，当你不得不长时间说话或身体的肾上腺素水平升高时你可能会开始颤抖，但这不应该让你虚弱。

你不断尝试，你就会擅长它，这就是以这种方式学习事物的人性。

回想一下您第一次骑自行车并将其与今天进行比较。 您现在可以无意识地这样做，因为您骑自行车的能力很高。

这就是你可以克服任何缺点的方法。 所以最后我得出结论
你应该擅长你所有的技能，然后你会很高兴！

请继续关注该系列第三部分的下一篇文章。
讨论必须继续！！ 保持快乐，保持周围环境清洁，
签署与 Bhalodiya 见面，

平安✌



原文:

What's up gals/guys !! Here I am with another post continuing the self-taught developer series.

In this section We will be answering "Is it good to be a Self Taught Developer ?". I said "WE" because I want you guys/gals to participate in the discussion 😊😉.

The upcoming points in this article are only my opinions and experiences, nothing more than that. All of you can also share your opinions in comment section.

To get answer of any question like this one, the best thing I do is list out advantages and drawbacks of the topic. SO lets do it.

Advantages of being a Self taught developer

Yup! things ain't easy when you are on your own. Still teaching myself:

  • Gave me sense of time.
  • Made me realize importance is discipline cuz that's what you need to be consistent.
  • Made me realize importance of consistency.
  • Feels like its never enough, and that my friend boosts your growth.(if taken positively 😅😅)
  • Most importantly Made me responsible for everything thing I type and everything I do.

Consistency can make you way more skillful than intensity ever would.

keep that fire lite everyday with same intensity.

Disadvantages of being a Self taught developer

Even if it has so many advantages it also has its own drawbacks:

  • Lack of friends.
  • Lack of huge network.
  • Lack of gals/guys who could help you out of any errors.

Communication

While these disadvantages may not seem severe at first glance, self-taught developers lack skills in communication and cooperation because of these minor disadvantages.

I thought communication shouldn't be a problem, anyone could do it but naahh, it's not as simple as it seems.

Communication is a very huge part of your job, because

  • It is one of the most important skill which will get you a job
  • Which will make other person think you are really confident of what you are speaking,
  • Which will make you different for others cuz not everyone has good communication skill.

Damn now it feels the disadvantages are really huge!

Solutions to disadvantages

Never be afraid to reinvent yourself

Even tough communication is a huge issue among most of the self-taught developers, it can be improved gradually.

There is nothing that can be learnt or improved.
Obviously some of the initial attempts might make you feel nervous ,you might start shaking when you have to talk for a long time or the adrenaline levels of body rises but that should not make you weak.

You keep trying and you get good at it, that's human nature of learning things in this way.

Think back to the first time you rode a bike and compare it to today. You can do this unconsciously now because of how good you are at riding a bike.

That's how you can overcome any drawbacks. So finally I conclude
that you should be good at all your skills and then you will be good to go!!

Stay tuned for the next post on the third part of the series.
The discussion must go on!! 😉

Stay happy, Keep your surroundings clean,
Signing off Meet Bhalodiya,

Peace ✌

item.title: 大数据 | Big Data
item.description:
大数据的定义是包含更多种类、数量增加且速度更快的数据。 这也被称为三个Vs。

简而言之，大数据是更大、更复杂的数据集，尤其是来自新数据源的数据。 这些数据集非常庞大，传统的数据处理软件根本无法管理它们。 但这些海量数据可用于解决您以前无法解决的业务问题。

一旦暂停，arjunsurendran0 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，arjunsurendran0 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，arjunsurendran0 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 arjunsurendran0 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

The definition of big data is data that contains greater variety, arriving in increasing volumes and with more velocity. This is also known as the three Vs.

Put simply, big data is larger, more complex data sets, especially from new data sources. These data sets are so voluminous that traditional data processing software just can’t manage them. But these massive volumes of data can be used to address business problems you wouldn’t have been able to tackle before.

In this Blog, we will see how to host a react JS app for free. There are so many ways to host your app online for free but in this blog we will see how to host on github pages.

Hosting a normal html, css and js files to github is very easy, but when it comes to react js it is a bit tricky.

Let's get started.

Step 1:

First step we need to do is to add Homepage to package.json

"homepage": "https://myusername.github.io/my-app",

it you want to host to github user page

"homepage": "https://myusername.github.io/",

Step 2:

The next step you need to do is to install gh-pages. to install run the following command in the terminal

npm install --save gh-pages

then add the following lines of code in your package.json scripts

"predeploy": "npm run build",
"deploy": "gh-pages -d build"

Step 3:

The very next step is to run the following command

Now you are done 💥💥

you have successfully hosted your react app on github 🚀

item.title: 干净的代码——我如何重构我的第一个项目 | Clean Code - How I'm Refactoring My First Project
item.description:
我一直在重构我的训练营 Phase - 1 项目。 这是我到目前为止所学和完成的。

动机

编码时，我脑海中最重复的问题之一是：“这是否允许？” 我在结构、规则和逻辑方面茁壮成长。 给我一个模板，我就走了，有条不紊地工作。

编码语言提供了一些这样的结构，但是有很多方法可以解决问题——哪种方法是正确的？ 从来没有正确的方法（除非您在编码训练营中并且通过测验的测试非常具体，感谢 Flatiron！），所以我按照别人的规则选择我的正确方法 - Robert C. Martin。

在其中，马丁认为混乱的代码是公司的垮台：“（由混乱的代码造成的）放缓程度可能很大。 在一两年的时间里，在项目开始时进展迅速的团队可能会以蜗牛的速度前进。 他们对代码所做的每一次更改都会破坏代码的其他两三个部分。 没有任何改变是微不足道的......随着混乱的加剧，团队的生产力继续下降，渐近接近于零...... [每个人] 团队中的 [is] 承受着提高生产力的可怕压力，因此他们制造了更多的混乱...... ”

在我之前的职业生涯中，我每天都在小型团队中构建 SQL 查询。 在 3-4 个月的项目过程中，我们的 SQL 查询变得非常复杂，调试其他人编写的脚本可能需要大量时间或部落知识。 在为期一周的项目结束了我的每个训练营阶段，我看到一个应用程序在两个开发人员之间建立复杂性的速度有多快。

到目前为止，通过阅读 3 章，发现本书中列出的规则在理论上很简单，但实施起来很复杂。 下面我写了一些我的最爱，以及我如何在我重构的代码中将它们付诸实践。

规则

在选择有意义的名字时...

每个概念选一个词

这个相当简单，并且已经为大量代码完成了，但我为我的任何服务器调用选择了 CRUD 动词。

当我想在页面中显示某些内容时决定“渲染”，等等。



使用可发音的名称

我们的代码库中也遵循了这个，但我喜欢他的例子，所以我把它放在这里：

不要使用带有变量名genymdhms的日期，在调试给其他开发人员时很难传递，而是使用generationTimestamp 。 或者代替modymdhms ，使用modificationTimestamp 。

在编写函数...

小的！

第二条规则是它们应该小于那个”

我将一个庞大的函数分解为更小的函数，它为代码的可读性创造了奇迹。

下面是之前，函数调用函数； 要求读者理解函数链以了解所有正在发生的事情：


这是之后，仍然很长但更具可读性：


做一件事

他们应该做得很好。 他们应该只这样做。”

读到这里，我点了点头，记下了。 但是当我开始更多地思考它时，什么是“一件事”？ 让我们从上面的 Small 中重新审视我的代码示例！ 部分; 我试图将其分解为以下 TO 段落：

为了显示所有电影详细信息，我们获取电影详细信息，然后渲染电影数据，然后获取电影的用户数据，然后渲染用户数据。

为了渲染用户电影的详细信息，我们渲染评分和观看状态，然后我们删除之前的电影评论，然后渲染当前的电影评论。

要呈现评分和观看状态...

你明白了。 但总的来说，当实现它时，它会产生易于阅读的代码。

使用描述性名称

我喜欢描述性的名字，我喜欢被鼓励写长的名字，但是什么时候太远了？ 让我们进行民意调查。

我喜欢解释论点的顺序和意图的想法。 这里有两个函数对两组数据有相似的用途。 将函数定义为合并就足够了吗？ 还是写 consolidateExistingAndNew 更清楚？ 我的第二个名字是不是有点矫枉过正？ 我喜欢它，因为它告诉我我的参数应该添加到函数中的顺序。 让我知道你的想法。

不知所措？ 我也是。 这里有两件事给了我希望：

这让我点头，并有助于将所有编码置于上下文中。

但是，这是一个很大的但是，他不会这样写，也不认为任何人都可以

这减轻了压力并激励我继续分析我的代码以帮助未来的我，以及其他任何查看我的代码的人。

再次值得一提 - 这些概念（包括生产力与时间的关系图）完全是 Robert C. Martin 在他的著作《清洁代码》中所记录的精彩作品，绝不是我自己的。 本文旨在描述我在他的工作中发现价值的过程，以及我如何尝试在自己的编码实践中实现它。

如果你有兴趣，这本书的 pdf 在这里： https ://yes-pdf.com/book/1038

快乐编码！



原文:

I’ve been refactoring my boot camp Phase - 1 project. Here’s what I’ve learned and completed so far.

The motivation

When coding, one of the most repeated questions in my head is: “Is this even allowed?” I thrive in structure, rules and logic. Give me a template, and I am off, working methodically.

Coding languages provide some of that structure, but there are so many ways to solve a problem - which is the right way? There is never a right way (unless you’re in a coding bootcamp and the tests to pass a quiz are very specific, thanks Flatiron!), so I’m choosing my right way by following someone else’s rules - Robert C. Martin.

Clean Code is a coding handbook I was recommended when I started my bootcamp. In it, Martin argues messy code is the downfall of companies: “The degree of the slowdown [by messy code] can be significant. Over the span of a year or two, teams that were moving fast in the beginning of a project can be moving at a snail’s pace. Every change they make to the code breaks two or three other parts of the code. No change is trivial...As the mess builds, the productivity of the team continues to decrease, asymptotically approaching zero...[Everyone] on the team [is] under horrific pressure to increase productivity so they make more messes...”

In my previous career, I worked in small teams building SQL queries everyday. Over the course of 3-4 month projects, our SQL queries became very complex and debugging scripts written by others could require significant time or tribal knowledge. In the week-long projects wrapping up each of my boot camp phases, I see how quickly an application builds complexity between two people developing.

Having read through 3 chapters so far, turns out the rules laid out in this book are simple in theory, but complicated to implement. Below I’ve written some of my favorites and how I’ve put them into practice in my refactored code.

The rules

In choosing meaningful names...

Pick One Word per Concept

“Pick one word for one abstract concept and stick with it”

This one is fairly easy and was already done for a good deal of the code but I choose the CRUD verb for any of my server calls.

Decided on “render” when I wanted something to show up in the page, etc.



Use Pronounceable Names

“It would be a shame not to take advantage of that huge portion of our brain that has evolved to deal with spoken language”

This one was also followed in our codebase but I like his example so I’ll put it here:

Instead of using a date with a variable name genymdhms that would be hard to relay when debugging to another developer, use generationTimestamp. Or instead of modymdhms, use modificationTimestamp.

In writing functions...

Small!

“The first rule of functions is that they should be small. The second rule is that they should be smaller than that”

I broke one behemoth of a function down into smaller functions and it did wonders for the code readability.

Below is before, functions calling functions; requiring the reader to understand the chain of functions to get an idea of what all is happening:


And here it is after, still long but much more readable:


Do One Thing

“Functions should do one thing. They should do it well. They should do it only.”

When I read this, I nodded my head and took note. But when I started to think about it more, what is “one thing?” Martin acknowledges this and elaborates: “we want to be able to read the program as though it were a set of TO paragraphs, each of which is describing the current level of abstraction and referencing subsequent TO paragraphs at the next level down”

Let’s revisit my code sample from the above Small! Section; I tried to break that down into TO paragraphs below:

To show all the movie details we get the movie details, then render the movie data, then we get the user data for the movie then we render the user data.

To render user movie details we render the rating and watch status, then we remove the previous movie comments, then render the current movie comments.

To render the rating and watch status...

You get the point. But overall, when implemented it results in easily readable code.

Use Descriptive Names

“A long descriptive name is better than a long descriptive comment”
“A function name can go a long way toward explaining the intent of the function and the order and intent of the arguments”

I like descriptive names, I like being encouraged to write long ones, but when is it too far? Let’s take a poll.

I liked the idea of explaining the order and intent of the arguments. Here are two functions that have similar purposes for two sets of data. Is it enough to define the function as coalesce? Or is it clearer to write consolidateExistingAndNew? Is my second name overkill? I like it because it tells me the order my arguments should be added to the function. Let me know what you think.

Overwhelmed? Me too. Here are two things that give me hope:

1 - Martin argues that the overall goal of code is “to tell the story of a system”

That gets my head nodding and helps put all coding into context.

2 - Martin admits to writing sloppy first drafts of code but then he massages and refines the code to eventually follow the rules he’s laid out. But, and it’s a big but, he doesn’t write that way to start and doesn’t think anyone could

That takes the pressure off and motivates me to continue analyzing my code to help out future me, and anyone else looking at my code.

It’s worth mentioning again - these concepts (including the productivity v. time graphic) are entirely Robert C. Martin’s wonderful work documented in his book Clean Code and are in no way my own. This article serves to describe my journey to finding the value in his work and how I attempt to implement it in my own coding practice.

If you are interested, a pdf of the book is here: https://yes-pdf.com/book/1038

Happy coding!
item.author: loopdeluce

item.title: TCP/IP | TCP/IP
item.description:
协议是一组规则，用于定义如何在计算机网络中连接的 2 个设备之间传输数据。其中一个流行的协议是 TCP/IP。

应用层

  • 传输层
  • 网络层
  • 数据链路层
  • 物理层

    • 它是 TCP/IP 协议的最顶层。它充当用户和模型之间的接口。它是唯一与用户交互的层。该层中定义的协议是 HTTPS、FTP、SMTP。用户应用程序在这些协议上工作。

    传输层将数据转换成更小的层，称为段，以便于传输数据。

    在 TCP 中还有一个确认过程来检查数据传输是否完成。如果传输未完成，则再次发送数据。

    数据被转换为数据包，以便于传输数据。 网络协议还负责分配路由协议。

    它还确定了这些数据包通过网络的路径。

    数据链路层负责用最近的路由器的 MAC 地址封装数据包，以及包含用于错误检测的校验和的报头链路。 它确保数据传输完全没有任何错误。

    如果发生任何错误，它会尝试解决它。

    它还负责将数据包转换为比特。它还监督通信是通过无线介质还是通过有线介质进行的。

    一旦暂停，ravi_sankar 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，ravi_sankar 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，ravi_sankar 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 ravi_sankar 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    TCP/IP
    TCP stands for transmission control protocol and IP stands for Internet Protocol. A protocol is a set of rules that define how data is transferred between 2 devices connected in a computer network.One such a popular protocol is TCP/IP.

    There are 5 layers in TCP/IP model:

    1. Application layer
    2. Transport layer
    3. Network layer
    4. Data-link layer
    5. Physical layer

    1. Application layer
    It is the top most layer of TCP/IP protocol.It acts as an interface between the user and the model.It is the only layer which interacts with the user.The protocols defined in the layer are HTTPS,FTP,SMTP.The user applications work on these protocols.

    2. Transport layer The protocols in this layer are TCP and UDP.It provides an end to end connection between 2 devices. TCP is a connection oriented protocol while UDP is a connectionless protocol.UDP is fast but not secure, while TCP is secure but slower. The transport layer converts the data into smaller layers called segments for easier transfer of data .

    Also in TCP there is an acknowledgement process to check whether the data transfer is completed or not.If the transfer is not completed the data is sent again.

    3. Network layer The protocols in the network layer are IP and ARP. IP stands for Internet Protocol.Each device has an unique IP address by which it is found on the network.It is responsible for delivering packets from the sender to the receiver’s end.Packets refers to small fragments of data that is to be transferred. The data is converted to packets for easier transfer of data. The network protocol is also responsible for assigning routing protocols .

    Also it determines the paths through the network for these packets to travel.

    4. Data-link layer

    The data link layer is responsible for encapsulating the packets with MAC address of the nearest router and also a header link which contains a checksum for error detection. It ensures that data transfer has occurred completely without any errors.

    If any errors have occurred it tries to resolve it.

    5. Physical Layer It is the lowest layer in the TCP/IP model.It is responsible for handling host to host connection between the sender and the receiver.

    Also it is responsible for converting packets into bits.It also oversees whether the communication occurs through wireless medium or through a wired medium.

    TCP/IP is the backbone of the internet .All the data that we send and receive are governed by the TCP/IP protocols .

    Once suspended, ravi_sankar will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, ravi_sankar will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by ravi_sankar will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If ravi_sankar is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: ravi_sankar

item.title: 使用 PNPM 工作区设置 Monorepo 并使用 Nx 加速它！ | Setup a Monorepo with PNPM workspaces and speed it up with Nx!
item.description:
在本文中，我们将深入探讨使用托管 Remix 应用程序和基于 React 的库的PNPM 工作区来设置新的 monorepo。 我们将学习如何使用 PNPM 运行命令，如何并行运行它们，最后我们将添加 Nx 以实现更复杂的任务调度，包括命令缓存等。

重要提示：如果您已经熟悉新 PNPM 工作区的设置和配置，请随时跳到本文后面我们添加 Nx 的部分。

更喜欢视频演练？

目录

初始化一个新的 PNPM 工作区

要开始使用，请确保您已安装 PNPM。 官方文档有一个安装页面，里面有详细的说明。 如果您必须处理多个不同版本的 NPM/PNPM 和节点版本，我还建议使用类似Volta的东西。

让我们创建一个名为pnpm-mono的新文件夹，将 cd 放入其中，然后运行pnpm init以生成顶级package.json 。 这将是我们的 PNPM monorepo 的根package.json 。

 ❯ mkdir pnpm-mono ❯ cd pnpm-mono ❯ pnpm init

初始化一个新的 Git 存储库可能也很方便，这样我们就可以在设置过程中提交和备份内容：

此时，让我们还创建一个.gitignore文件以立即排除诸如node_modules和通用构建输出文件夹之类的内容。

 # .gitignore node_modules dist build

设置 Monorepo 结构

以包为中心的存储库，用于开发和发布一组有凝聚力的可重用包。 这是开源世界中的常见设置，可以在Angular 、 React 、 Vue等存储库中看到。 这些 repos 的特点是最常见的是具有一个packages文件夹，然后通常将其发布到一些公共注册表，例如NPM 。

  • 以应用程序为中心的存储库，主要用于开发应用程序和产品。 这是公司常见的设置。 此类存储库的特点是具有apps和packages或libs文件夹，其中apps文件夹包含可构建和可部署的应用程序，而packages或libs文件夹包含特定于在 monorepo 中开发的一个或多个应用程序的库。 您仍然可以将其中一些库发布到公共注册表。

    • 在本文中，我们将使用“以应用程序为中心”的方法，来演示我们如何拥有一个从 monorepo 中使用包的应用程序。

    在pnpm-mono中创建一个apps和packages文件夹：

    现在让我们配置 PNPM 以正确识别 monorepo 工作区。 基本上我们必须在存储库的根目录创建一个pnpm-workspace.yaml文件，定义我们的 monorepo 结构：

     # pnpm-workspace.yaml packages : # executable/launchable applications - ' apps/*' # all packages in subdirs of packages/ and components/ - ' packages/*'

    添加混音应用程序

    我们现在应该准备好添加我们的第一个应用程序。 对于这个例子，我选择了Remix ，但你可以在这里真正托管任何类型的应用程序，这并不重要。

    信息：我们在这里使用正常的Remix 安装和设置过程，您可以在他们的文档页面上找到。

    由于我们希望将应用程序放在apps文件夹中，因此我们需要cd进入它：

     ❯ cd apps ❯ npx create-remix@latest

    系统将要求您输入应用名称。 让我们使用“my-remix-app”，我们将在本文的其余部分使用它。 显然可以随意使用不同的。 此外，Remix 设置过程还将询问您几个自定义确切设置的问题。 此处的特定选项与我们的文章并不真正相关，因此请随意选择最适合您需求的选项。

    您现在应该在apps/my-remix-app文件夹或您选择的任何名称中拥有一个 Remix 应用程序。 Remix has already a package.json with corresponding scripts configured: Remix 已经有一个package.json配置了相应的脚本：

     { "private" : true , "sideEffects" : false , "scripts" : { "build" : "remix build" , "dev" : "remix dev" , "start" : "remix-serve build" }, ...
}

    通常，在 monorepo 中，您希望从存储库的根目录运行命令，而不必经常在文件夹之间切换。 现在（在撰写本文时）碰巧 Remix 的默认package.json没有定义 PNPM 想要运行包的name属性。 所以让我们在apps/my-remix-app/package.json中定义一个：

     { "name" : "my-remix-app" , "private" : true , "sideEffects" : false , ...
}

    您现在应该可以使用以下命令在开发模式下为您的 Remix 应用程序提供服务：

     ❯ pnpm --filter my-remix-app dev

    创建共享 UI 库

    现在我们已经设置了我们的应用程序，让我们创建一个可以被我们的应用程序使用的库包。

     ❯ cd packages ❯ mkdir shared-ui

    接下来，让我们创建一个包含以下内容的package.json （您也可以使用pnpm init并对其进行调整）：

     { "private" : true , "name" : "shared-ui" , "description" : "Shared UI components" , "scripts" : {}, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "dependencies" : { }, "devDependencies" : { }
}

    请注意，我们将其声明为private ，因为我们不想将其发布到 NPM 或其他地方，而只是在我们的工作区中本地引用和使用它。 我还删除了version属性，因为它没有被使用。

    作为我选择使用React （因此我们可以在 Remix 中导入它）和TypeScript （因为它现在几乎可以被视为标准）的技术堆栈。 让我们从工作空间的根目录安装这些依赖项：

     ❯ pnpm add --filter shared-ui react ❯ pnpm add --filter shared-ui typescript -D

    通过将--filter shared-ui传递给安装命令，我们将这些 NPM 包本地安装到shared-ui库中。

    信息：请注意，如果库包和使用者（例如我们的应用程序）使用的 React/TypeScript 版本不同，这可能会导致版本冲突。 采用单一版本策略，将包移动到 monoreopo 的根目录，是一种可能的解决方案。

    我们的第一个组件将是一个非常简单的Button组件。 所以让我们创建一个：

     // packages/shared-ui/Button.tsx import * as React from ' react ' ; export function Button ( props : any ) { return < button onClick = { () => props . onClick () } > { props . children } </ button >; } export default Button ;

    我们还希望有一个公共 API，我们可以在其中导出要在shared-ui包之外使用的组件：

     // packages/shared-ui/index.tsx export * from ' ./Button ' ;

    为了简单起见，我们只使用 TypeScript 编译器来编译我们的包。 我们可以有一些更复杂的设置来将多个文件捆绑在一起等，比如Rollup或您喜欢使用的任何东西，但这超出了本文的范围。

    要创建所需的编译输出，请使用以下配置创建packages/shared-ui/tsconfig.json文件。

     { "compilerOptions" : { "jsx" : "react-jsx" , "allowJs" : true , "esModuleInterop" : true , "allowSyntheticDefaultImports" : true , "module" : "commonjs" , "outDir" : "./dist" }, "include" : [ "." ], "exclude" : [ "dist" , "node_modules" , "**/*.spec.ts" ]
}

    在 monorepo 中，最好将公共配置部分提取到更高级别的配置中（例如在根目录中），然后在各种项目中扩展它。 这是为了避免在各种 monorepo 包中出现大量重复。 为了简单起见，我将它们全部保存在一个地方。

    如您所见， outDir指向包本地dist文件夹。 所以我们应该在shared-ui包的package.json中添加一个主入口点：

     { "private" : true , "name" : "shared-ui" , "main" : "dist/index.js" ,
}

    最后，实际构建包括从之前的输出中删除一些残留文件夹，然后调用 TypeScript 编译器 ( tsc )。 这是完整的packages/shared-ui/package.json文件：

     { "private" : true , "name" : "shared-ui" , "description" : "Shared UI components" , "main" : "dist/index.js" , "scripts" : { "build" : "rm -rf dist && tsc" }, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "dependencies" : { "react" : "^17.0.2" }, "devDependencies" : { "typescript" : "^4.6.4" }
}

    使用以下命令从 PNPM 工作区的根目录运行构建：

     ❯ pnpm --filter shared-ui build

    如果构建成功，您应该在packages/shared-ui/dist文件夹中看到编译后的输出。

    从 Remix 应用程序使用我们的 shared-ui 包

    我们的shared-ui库已准备就绪，因此我们可以在存储库的apps文件夹中托管的 Remix 应用程序中使用它。 我们可以手动添加依赖到 Remix 的package.json或者使用 PNPM 添加它：

     ❯ pnpm add shared-ui --filter my-remix-app --workspace

    这会将其添加到apps/my-remix-app/package.json的依赖项中：

     { "name" : "my-remix-app" , "private" : true , "sideEffects" : false , ... "dependencies" : { ... "shared-ui" : "workspace:*" }, ...
}

    如果您使用外部 NPM 包，则使用特定版本真的很有意义。

    为了使用我们的Button组件，我们现在从一些 Remix 路由中导入它。 将apps/my-remix-app/app/routes/index.tsx的内容替换为以下内容：

     // apps/my-remix-app/app/routes/index.tsx import { Button } from ' shared-ui ' ; export default function Index () { return ( < div > < Button onClick = { () => console . log ( ' clicked ' ) } > Click me </ Button > </ div > ); }

    如果您现在再次运行 Remix 应用程序，您应该会看到正在呈现的按钮。

     ❯ pnpm --filter my-remix-app dev

    如果你碰巧遇到如下错误，那是因为你需要先构建shared-ui

     Error: Cannot find module '/Users/juri/nrwl/content/pnpm-demos/pnpm-mono/apps/my-remix-app/node_modules/shared-ui/dist/index.js'. Please verify that the package.json has a valid "main" entry  at tryPackage (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:353:19)  at Function.Module._findPath (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:566:18)  at Function.Module._resolveFilename (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:919:27)  at Function.Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:778:27)  at Module.require (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1005:19)  at require (node:internal/modules/cjs/helpers:102:18)  at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/juri/nrwl/content/pnpm-demos/pnpm-mono/apps/my-remix-app/app/routes/index.tsx:1:24)  at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1105:14)  at Object.Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1159:10)  at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:981:32)

    要构建它，请运行

    ❯ pnpm --filter shared-ui build

    为什么？ 这是由于 PNPM 创建的符号链接，以便能够引用和解决本地依赖关系。 通过将shared-ui: "workspace:*"添加到 Remix 的package.json中，您可以指示 PNPM 将符号链接添加到 Remix 的node_modules文件夹。

    使用 PNPM 运行命令

    我们已经看到了如何使用--filter对单个包的命令范围进行限制：
